findings.title: Capacity-wait reconciliation preserves claim fencing on provider failures
findings.details: Tracker refresh failure keeps all waiting claims; heartbeat failure retains the valid owned waiter; remote release failure retains local claimed, capacity-waiting, and repository-reservation state, preventing unsafe concurrent dispatch.

findings.title: Ownership transfer is handled without mutating the foreign remote claim
findings.details: When the refreshed runId differs, reconciliation removes only the stale local reservation and does not heartbeat or remotely release the new owner's claim.

findings.title: Exact-commit regression and quality evidence is affirmative
findings.details: Tests cover missing, terminal, unroutable, foreign-owned, and valid-owned waiters, including same-repository dispatch and preservation of attempt 3 with an empty retry map. Exact-commit evidence reports 363 tests passing, 100.00% coverage, strict Credo and Dialyzer clean.

findings.title: Reviewed commit identity remains bound
findings.details: Latest durable clean-worktree evidence confirms local HEAD 40750c297ed42435dabe1d4670bfda85ce92af36; an independent live GitHub comparison reports bos/issue-51 identical to that SHA.

summary: Passed security review for exact commit 40750c297ed42435dabe1d4670bfda85ce92af36. Capacity-wait reconciliation safely releases stale reservations, preserves fencing on provider failures, avoids touching foreign claims, and maintains attempt/retry invariants with exact-commit regression and full-gate evidence.
